Set up the multi-worker cluster environment with 2 nodes, each having 2 GPUs; allocate resources using: salloc --nodes=2 --time=20:00:00 --gres=gpu:2 --partition=gpu --exclusive
On node 1 (gpu1): clone or access the TensorFlow Model Garden repo at /home/cdacapp1/scaling/models-master
On node 1 (gpu1): set PYTHONPATH to include /home/cdacapp1/scaling/models-master
On node 1 (gpu1): set DATA_DIR to /scratch/cdacapp1/sowmya/actual_imagenet/tf_records containing ImageNet TFRecords
On node 1 (gpu1): set MODEL_DIR to /home/cdacapp1/scaling/models-master/official/legacy/image_classification/checkpointss
On node 1 (gpu1): activate Python 3.9.7 environment with TensorFlow-GPU 2.11.0 and CUDA 11.0 (using conda environment tf11)
On node 1 (gpu1): prepare config file gpu.yaml with these runtime parameters: distribution_strategy: 'multi_worker_mirrored', worker_hosts: 'ip1:6655,ip2:6655', num_gpus: 2, task_index: 0

On node 1 (gpu1): run training with the command: python3 classifier_trainer.py --mode=train_and_eval --model_type=resnet --dataset=imagenet --model_dir=$MODEL_DIR --data_dir=$DATA_DIR --config_file=configs/examples/resnet/imagenet/gpu.yaml
On node 2 (gpu2): replicate the environment setup steps identical to node 1 (clone repo, set PYTHONPATH, DATA_DIR, MODEL_DIR, activate conda tf11 environment)
On node 2 (gpu2): prepare config file gpu1.yaml with runtime parameters: distribution_strategy: 'multi_worker_mirrored', worker_hosts: 'ip1:6655,ip2:6655', num_gpus: 2, task_index: 1

On node 2 (gpu2): run training with the command: python3 classifier_trainer.py --mode=train_and_eval --model_type=resnet --dataset=imagenet --model_dir=$MODEL_DIR --data_dir=$DATA_DIR --config_file=configs/examples/resnet/imagenet/gpu1.yaml
Verify that the training processes on both nodes establish communication over port 6655 and TensorFlow cluster resolver correctly identifies the two workers
Monitor GPU utilization on both nodes to confirm that all 4 GPUs (2 per node) are active during training
Record throughput metrics (examples/sec) from training logs on both nodes; note throughput on a single GPU run (~270 examples/sec) and multi-GPU single node run (~700 examples/sec)
Assert that the multi-node multi-GPU run throughput does not exceed approximately 270 examples/sec, indicating no scaling across nodes
Optionally, add debug prints or logging in classifier_trainer.py to output the effective number of replicas detected by tf.distribute.MultiWorkerMirroredStrategy
Confirm that batch size per replica and global batch size settings match expected values for 4 GPUs total (e.g., if per GPU batch size is 32, global batch size should be 128)

Validate that the environment variables TF_CONFIG or equivalent cluster configuration is not explicitly set (since the code uses worker_hosts and task_index in config file), and that this might contribute to cluster setup issues
Check network connectivity and firewall rules between ip1 and ip2 on port 6655 to ensure workers can communicate
Optionally, run a simplified minimal TensorFlow multi-worker mirrored strategy example on the same cluster setup to verify cluster communication works outside the Model Garden code
Collect and save logs from both nodes, including TensorFlow distributed strategy initialization logs and throughput logs for post-mortem analysis
